title: Tokyo Tea Titration Lab
description: A miniature chemistry lab set within the LittlestTokyo cityscape where the user performs a multi-step acid-base titration using translucent teacups as reaction vessels. Players must carefully measure, mix, and neutralize reagents across multiple teacup stations while obeying conservation of mass, with full undo/redo support for each step.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Load LittlestTokyo.glb as the environment backdrop scaled to 0.004 and centered, representing a miniature city lab bench. Load DiffuseTransmissionTeacup.glb five times at different positions along a curved rail in front of the city, each scaled to 3.0, acting as labeled reaction vessels: Acid Stock (red), Base Stock (blue), Indicator Cup (purple), Mixing Vessel (clear), and Waste Vessel (gray). Use a PerspectiveCamera at (0,8,12) looking at origin, with hemisphere light (sky 0xffffff, ground 0x444444) plus a directional light casting soft shadows. Enable OrbitControls with damping. Implement a reagent system tracking volume (mL) and concentration (mol/L) per cup using conservation of mass: total moles across all cups must remain constant. Create a left-side HTML panel with buttons: Transfer (select source then destination cup by clicking), Set Volume slider (1-25 mL), Add Indicator (adds 2 mL indicator to selected cup), Heat (doubles reaction rate in selected cup, changes liquid color warmth), Cool (halves rate, shifts color cool), Stir (triggers spin animation on cup), Measure pH (displays calculated pH as floating 3D text above cup), Undo, and Redo. Each transfer animates a colored arc particle stream between cups. Liquid level inside each teacup is shown via a flat translucent disc mesh whose Y-position and color update reactively. pH is computed from net H+ concentration; indicator cup liquid changes from red (pH<4) through green (pH~7) to blue (pH>10). The goal displayed at top: neutralize the Mixing Vessel to pH 7.0±0.3 using exactly the right volumes. Track all actions in a history stack supporting at least 14 undo/redo steps. Highlight the active cup with a glowing outline pass. Show a success confetti particle burst and city animation play when target pH is achieved. Minimum 10 interaction steps required: select Acid, transfer to Mixing, select Base, transfer to Mixing, add Indicator to Mixing, stir, measure pH, adjust with more base, heat, measure again, cool, confirm neutralization.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  totalMolesAcid: number,         // total moles of acid across all vessels (conserved)
  totalMolesBase: number,         // total moles of base across all vessels (conserved)
  mixingVesselVolume: number,         // current volume in mL in the mixing vessel
  mixingVesselPH: number,         // calculated pH of mixing vessel solution
  indicatorAdded: boolean,         // whether indicator has been added to mixing vessel
  transferCount: number,         // count of successful transfer operations performed
  heatCycleCount: number,         // number of times heat button was applied
  stirAnimationActive: boolean,         // whether stir animation is currently running
  neutralizationAchieved: boolean,         // whether pH is within neutral range (6.8-7.2)
  wasteVesselVolume: number,         // accumulated volume in mL in waste vessel
  reactionRateMultiplier: number,         // current reaction rate multiplier (0.5, 1.0, 2.0)
  selectedCupIndex: number,         // index of currently selected cup (0-4)
}
